summary: P0/P1 demo rails are built locally. H1 and E1 P2 safety gates are verified locally. Real voice server session plumbing, browser WebRTC attach code, transcript persistence, Sandy tool gateway routing, the no-PHI local voice red-team harness, live voice preflight command, and browser live-latency metrics hook are built behind off-by-default flags. The E2 wrong-patient local identity-corroboration gate is verified and wired into the local claims-ingest seam. The H2 local async access gate is verified with patient/pack-scoped short-lived tokens and broad-grant rejection. The H3 local Part 2 suppression gate is verified with sensitive facility/category stripping and fail-closed unknown dispositions. The H4 local break-glass gate is verified with request/approval, TTL, expired-read blocking, post-hoc review, Part 2 purpose-consent, adolescent purpose-consent, guardian-proxy blocking, and category-match enforcement. The H5 local SMS disclosure gate is verified with approved EN/ES templates, deterministic slots, category exclusions, and disclosure linting. The D2 local adolescent consent gate is verified with Kentucky PHQ/GAD minor-consent floors, QMHP unaccompanied-youth verification, under-16 fail-closed behavior, substance-use self-consent, guardian proxy suppression, and clinician health-benefit override. The D4 local PDC diabetes adherence gate is verified with PDC-DR treatment-period math, diabetes all-class grouping, same-drug overlap carry-forward, insulin exclusion, unknown-drug review, and claims-floor refill-gap separation. The P3 local ingestion boundary is verified with registered source vocabulary, patient-access claims consent checks, FHIR provenance requirements, and E2/H2/H3 gate composition. The P5 local device boundary is verified with registered device sources, canonical unit checks, FHIR provenance, non-diagnostic insight summaries, unsafe action blocking, web/native gating, and the local D4 PDC policy. Live Realtime audio red-team and live p95/p99 latency proof remain pending. Real-PHI pilot remains blocked by production MPI/FHIR/HIE integration, production consent/RLS, production Part 2, production SMS, real device integrations, and deployment-substrate controls.

workstreams.name: Retinopathy protocol state engine
workstreams.notes: Prototype-specific engine exists; production pack-parameterized rail is not built.

workstreams.name: Navigator queue
workstreams.notes: Demo queue exists; production single navigator_tasks table is not built.

workstreams.name: Source fact provenance shape
workstreams.notes: Seed provenance shape exists; FHIR-backed provenance is not built.

workstreams.name: Health alerts simulation
workstreams.notes: Static/pure simulation only; no real device or outreach scheduler.

workstreams.name: P5 device rail boundary
workstreams.notes: Local rail accepts registered device readings only with active consent, canonical units, and FHIR references; accepted readings land as device source facts; deterministic insights remain non-diagnostic; unsafe dosing/diagnosis/med-change actions are blocked; HealthKit/Health Connect stay disabled on web. The local D4 PDC diabetes policy/calculation is verified separately with npm run d4:gate. Real native/device/API/FHIR-store integrations and production pharmacy-claims PDC feeds are not built.

workstreams.name: D4 PDC diabetes adherence gate
workstreams.notes: Local policy follows the CMS 2026 QRS/PQA Diabetes All Class PDC-DR shape: IPSD-to-measurement-year-end treatment period, 80 percent threshold, diabetes all-class medication grouping, same-target-drug overlap carry-forward, insulin exclusion, unknown medication review, and claims-floor refill-gap event separation. Production still needs real pharmacy claims feeds, official value-set refresh/import controls, and FHIR-store writes before PDC can be used as a live adherence metric.

workstreams.name: Kentucky SDOH resource finder
workstreams.notes: Prototype resource flow; production directory ownership and refresh cadence remain open.

workstreams.name: P2 deterministic safety gate
workstreams.notes: H1 and E1 are closed for the local P2 safety gate. Production alert transport still belongs to deployment substrate work before any real-PHI pilot.

workstreams.name: Real voice and tool gateway
workstreams.notes: Safety gate is verified. Server-minted OpenAI Realtime client-secret route exists behind RHTP_REAL_VOICE. Browser WebRTC attach code exists behind NEXT_PUBLIC_RHTP_REAL_VOICE/VITE_RHTP_REAL_VOICE and cleans up microphone tracks on failed SDP exchange. Successful Realtime session mints create voice_sessions rows, completed transcript events persist as transcript_segments, and Realtime function calls route through the server-gated Sandy tool gateway into tool_calls plus audit_events with model/version/session/tool/pack provenance. The no-PHI local voice red-team harness proves the why-me to confirmed-plan journey, five P2 adversarial cases, gateway mutation coverage, audit provenance coverage, and local synthetic latency. The live preflight command checks flags and credentials and can time the provider client-secret mint only when RHTP_LIVE_VOICE_PROVIDER_MINT=1 is explicitly set. Browser live sessions now expose getMetricsReport() for Realtime speech-start to output-audio latency and tool-gateway samples. Live Realtime audio red-team and live p95/p99 latency proof remain pending. Flags remain off.

workstreams.name: FHIR/claims/HIE ingestion
workstreams.notes: Local P3 source registry and patient-access claims boundary are verified. Registered-source claims require active consent and FHIR references, route through the E2 identity gate, hold wrong-patient claims for review, and compose the local H2/H3 gates. This is still not a real Blue Button, MCO, KHIE, TEFCA, or Medplum integration.

workstreams.name: E2 identity corroboration and claims ingest seam
workstreams.notes: Local gate downgrades deterministic strong-ID-only matches to identity_match_review, holds claims facts when identity review is required, and lands corroborated claims facts as patientConfirmed=false without protocol events or autonomous outreach. E2 is not fully closed until the same behavior is proven through production MPI/FHIR/HIE adapters.

workstreams.name: H2 async RLS token gate
workstreams.notes: Local gateway mints short-lived bearer tokens, stores only hashes, scopes reads to one patient and concrete pack ids, blocks cross-patient/cross-pack/expired/revoked access, audits every decision, and rejects wildcard broad grants. H2 remains open for real-PHI until production consent repository claims and database RLS policies enforce the same contract.

workstreams.name: H3 Part 2 facility suppression gate
workstreams.notes: Local HIE discharge ingest strips sensitive facility/category wording before source facts, navigator queue text, audit details, and route responses; sensitive evidence becomes generic segmented-data review; unknown dispositions fail closed without source facts. H3 remains open for real-PHI until production HIE/FHIR adapters, storage policies, and outbound surfaces enforce the same rule.

workstreams.name: H5 SMS disclosure gate
workstreams.notes: Local SMS render path uses approved EN/ES templates, deterministic slots, category exclusion, disclosure linting, and audit events. H5 remains open for production SMS until A2P registration, opt-out handling, sender controls, delivery telemetry, and live flagging are complete.

workstreams.name: H4 break-glass consent gate
workstreams.notes: Local rail proves request, approval, TTL, expired-read blocking, post-hoc review, Part 2 purpose-consent requirement, adolescent purpose-consent requirement, guardian-proxy blocking for adolescent facts, and category-match enforcement before segmented reads. H4 remains open for real-PHI until production consent repository, segmented storage/RBAC/RLS, review workflow, and legal/clinical owner sign-off are complete.

workstreams.name: D2 adolescent consent and proxy disclosure gate
workstreams.notes: Local policy pins Kentucky minor-consent and proxy rules for PHQ/GAD and SDOH packs: age-16 physician outpatient mental-health self-consent, age-16 unaccompanied-youth QMHP path with verification attempt, under-16 fail-closed behavior without guardian consent, substance-use self-consent, default guardian proxy suppression for minor-consented confidential data, clinician health-benefit parent-notice override, and general SDOH guardian-proxy allowance. Production still needs legal/clinical owner sign-off and real consent/RBAC/RLS enforcement.

workstreams.name: Deployment substrate
workstreams.notes: Status tracking exists. IaC, VPC, secrets, observability, and synthetic-PHI staging are not built.

blockers.title: Model-in-the-crisis-path
blockers.requiredControl: Versioned deterministic recall floor, maintained adversarial corpus, model-net-only hits hard-lock and create rule-gap tickets, and fail-safe alerting when the model net degrades.
blockers.residual: Closed for local P2 safety gate. Production alert transport remains deployment-substrate work before any real-PHI pilot.

blockers.title: Silent wrong-patient linkage
blockers.requiredControl: Strong-ID matches must corroborate DOB and/or name, downgrade mismatches to navigator review, and block autonomous outreach until patient confirmation.
blockers.residual: Local claims ingest seam and P3 patient-access claims boundary are verified. MPI ownership is locally decided as own MPI plus multi-field corroboration. E2 still blocks real-PHI until production MPI/FHIR/claims/HIE adapters and production storage/RLS/Part 2 controls prove the same downgrade/no-outreach behavior.

blockers.title: Grounding verifier is a stub
blockers.requiredControl: Deterministic block layer using regex, lexicon, numeric normalization, and store-diff; model judge can only add blocks.

blockers.title: Async lane RLS bypass
blockers.requiredControl: Gateway-minted single-patient, pack-scoped, short-lived token for every async job; no standing broad async grant.
blockers.residual: Local token gate is verified. Still blocks real-PHI until production consent repository claims, database RLS policies, and async job infrastructure prove the same single-patient/pack-scoped behavior.

blockers.title: Part 2 leakage through facility identity
blockers.phaseGate: Before P3 HIE and P7 BH packs
blockers.requiredControl: Suppress sensitive facility identity before pack, insight, navigator, or outbound exposure; fail closed on unrecognized disposition.
blockers.residual: Local Part 2 suppression is verified. Still blocks real-PHI until production HIE/FHIR adapters, storage policies, and outbound patient/navigator surfaces prove the same suppression and fail-closed behavior.

blockers.title: Break-glass is not Part 2 consent
blockers.requiredControl: Define issuance, approval, TTL, mandatory review, audit event, and purpose-specific Part 2 consent.
blockers.residual: Local break-glass control is verified. Still blocks real-PHI/P7 until production consent repository, segmented storage/RBAC/RLS, review workflow, and production legal/clinical owner sign-off are complete.

blockers.title: SMS condition-name leakage
blockers.requiredControl: Approved template library, deterministic slotting, disclosure linting in every language, and category exclusion.
blockers.residual: Local SMS disclosure gate is verified. Still blocks production SMS until A2P registration, opt-out handling, sender controls, delivery telemetry, and live environment flagging are complete.

blockers.title: MPI ownership
blockers.requiredControl: Choose own MPI, KHIE MPI, or payer member-id approach; default is own MPI plus multi-field corroboration.
blockers.residual: Closed as a local operating decision: own MPI plus multi-field corroboration. Production MPI implementation remains future work.

blockers.title: Adolescent confidential consent model
blockers.requiredControl: Pin Kentucky minor-consent ages and proxy disclosure rules before PHQ/GAD and SDOH packs.
blockers.residual: Closed as a local operating decision using current Kentucky statutory sources and HHS HIPAA proxy guidance. Production still requires Kentucky counsel and clinical owner sign-off plus consent repository, proxy-portal, RBAC/RLS, and audit enforcement before PHQ/GAD or adolescent SDOH use with real PHI.

blockers.title: Longitudinal instance granularity
blockers.requiredControl: Choose one long-lived instance per patient/pack or rolling intervals; default is long-lived instance plus high-frequency insight lane.
blockers.residual: Closed as a local operating decision: one long-lived patient/pack instance plus high-frequency insight append lane. Production event-storage implementation remains future work.

blockers.title: PDC window and drug-class grouping
blockers.requiredControl: Match the HEDIS/RHTP pdc_diabetes definition exactly.
blockers.residual: Closed as a local operating decision against the CMS 2026 QRS/PQA PDC-DR contract. Production still requires official value-set refresh controls, live pharmacy claims feeds, FHIR-store writes, and measure-owner sign-off before reporting.

blockers.title: CGM stream-vs-summary rule boundary
blockers.requiredControl: Choose which rules read raw Timescale streams versus FHIR summaries; default is raw per-night for nocturnal_hyperglycemia.
blockers.residual: Closed as a local operating decision: raw per-night CGM for nocturnal pattern rules and daily summaries for TIR-style rules. Production stream storage remains future work.

blockers.title: Native shell timing
blockers.requiredControl: Confirm Capacitor shell ships with the device rail, not earlier.
blockers.residual: Closed as a local operating decision: native shell starts at P5 with the device rail. Device rail implementation remains future work.

featureFlags.flipCondition: Real voice tool gateway, transcript storage, grounding verifier, and red-team gate pass.
Server flag is on and the browser bundle has been redeployed.
Claims/FHIR ingest, identity corroboration, consent, RLS, provenance, and Part 2 controls pass.
Template library, disclosure lint, opt-out, A2P path, and delivery telemetry pass.
Native shell, device consent, source labeling, and no-dosing guardrails pass.
A second pack ships as configuration and content with zero new rail code.
All real-PHI gates close, BAAs are recorded, and clinical safety sign-off is recorded.

nextActions: Run npm run ops:status before claiming current project state.
Run npm run safety:gate before claiming P2 safety-gate proof.
Run npm run identity:gate before claiming local E2 wrong-patient control proof.
Run npm run async:gate before claiming local H2 async access proof.
Run npm run part2:gate before claiming local H3 Part 2 suppression proof.
Run npm run sms:gate before claiming local H5 SMS disclosure proof.
Run npm run d2:gate before claiming local D2 adolescent consent/proxy proof.
Run npm run p3:gate before claiming local P3 ingestion-boundary proof.
Run npm run d4:gate before claiming local D4 PDC diabetes adherence proof.
Run npm run p5:gate before claiming local P5 device-boundary proof.
Run npm run h4:gate before claiming local H4 break-glass proof.

Run npm run voice:live:preflight in a no-PHI environment with RHTP_REAL_VOICE, NEXT_PUBLIC_RHTP_REAL_VOICE or VITE_RHTP_REAL_VOICE, and OPENAI_API_KEY set; set RHTP_LIVE_VOICE_PROVIDER_MINT=1 only when provider timing is intended.

Run the live Realtime audio retinopathy voice journey red-team with RHTP_REAL_VOICE and NEXT_PUBLIC_RHTP_REAL_VOICE enabled in a no-PHI browser environment, then record getMetricsReport().voiceTurnP95Ms and voiceTurnP99Ms.
Measure live voice p95 and p99 latency before claiming P2 real voice exit.
Extend the E2 identity gate from the local claims ingest seam into production MPI/FHIR/HIE adapters before closing E2 for real-PHI.
Extend the H2 async token gate into production consent repository claims, database RLS policies, and async job workers before closing H2 for real-PHI.
Extend the H3 Part 2 suppression gate into production HIE/FHIR adapters, storage policies, and outbound surfaces before closing H3 for real-PHI.
Extend the H4 break-glass gate into production consent repository, segmented storage/RBAC/RLS, post-hoc review workflow, and production legal/clinical owner sign-off before closing H4 for real-PHI.
Extend the H5 SMS disclosure gate into production A2P registration, opt-out handling, sender controls, delivery telemetry, and live flagging before closing H5 for production SMS.
Build real Blue Button, Kentucky MCO Patient Access, KHIE, and Medplum adapter proof before claiming P3 production readiness.
Build real native shell, HealthKit/Health Connect, Dexcom OAuth/API, production pharmacy claims PDC feeds, stream storage, and FHIR-store writes before claiming P5 production readiness.
Import and version official PQA/CMS value sets before reporting live pdc_diabetes outcomes.
Assign owners for KHIE engagement, first Medicaid MCO, and model BAAs before real P3 execution.
